items.criterion: The PR implements KUSABI-CORE-MVP-MEMORY-BINDING-ARCHIVE-CLEANUP-001 and does not substitute runtime remediation, local cleanup execution, migration, release, or publish work.
All changed files remain within the Cell allowed paths, with no src, package, workflow, deploy, local config, DB content, docs/v2, schema, fixture, runner, branch protection, or ruleset edits.
The Cell anchors to issue #228 corrected policy: retained connected/development agents use shared PostgreSQL, stale aliases are inactive/archive, and SQLite exceptions remain FX-only.
The cleanup contract is limited to lead-ama, lead-sus, and lead-tuk stale SQLite binding residue and does not touch retained active agents or FX exceptions.
Retired, deleted, replaced, duplicate, or stale aliases remain inactive/archive targets and are not revived through migration work or approved as SQLite exceptions.
The contract requires preserving evidence that lead-ama was retired/replaced_by codex-aun before any local cleanup can be considered handled.
The PR does not commit .mcp.json, Claude/Codex settings, env files, secrets, local DB files, or stored memory content.
The Cell prohibits stored memory text, secrets, local DB content, export, reveal, deletion, purge, rewrite, migration, and semantic inspection from this PR.
Diff checks and changed YAML/JSON parse evidence are present and match the reported scope.
The PR does not authorize backend parity, common DB completion, UAMP conformance, compliance, federation, release, publish, DLP, zero-leakage, or legal/regulatory claims.
